clusters.title: Trains halted across Germany by communication system problem
clusters.neutral_headline: Germany rail network briefly halted nationwide due to IT malfunction
bullet_summary: Reported by Reuters Top News, BBC World, Sky News World and others

items.title: Trains halted across Germany by communication system problem
items.standfirst: A nationwide failure in Deutsche Bahn’s GSM-R rail communications system briefly stopped all trains across Germany, stranding passengers and forcing delays at stations. Service later resumed step by step after the outage was resolved. [apnews.com]
items.content: A nationwide failure in Deutsche Bahn’s GSM-R rail communications system briefly stopped all trains across Germany, stranding passengers and forcing delays at stations. Service later resumed step by step after the outage was resolved. [apnews.com]

items.title: Germany rail network briefly halted nationwide due to IT malfunction
items.standfirst: Rail company Deutsche Bahn had to pause train services across the country for more than two-and-a-half hours.
items.content: Rail company Deutsche Bahn had to pause train services across the country for more than two-and-a-half hours.

items.title: Trains across Germany briefly halted after communication system issue
items.standfirst: Germany's railway system was brought to a halt on Tuesday evening, leaving passengers stranded across the country following an IT issue.
items.content: Germany's railway system was brought to a halt on Tuesday evening, leaving passengers stranded across the country following an IT issue.

items.title: German Rail Services Resume After Nationwide Radio Failure
items.standfirst: Germany’s national rail operator resumed most services on Wednesday morning after a communications system outage brought trains to a nationwide standstill late Tuesday.
items.content: Germany’s national rail operator resumed most services on Wednesday morning after a communications system outage brought trains to a nationwide standstill late Tuesday.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Ronaldo becomes first player to score in six editions
items.standfirst: Scoring a brace against Uzbekistan on Tuesday, Cristiano Ronaldo made history by becoming the first player to score in six different editions of the FIFA World Cup. The 41-year-old will hope to secure Portugal a knockout stage berth, with the squad facing Colombia for their final group stage match on Saturday.
items.content: Scoring a brace against Uzbekistan on Tuesday, Cristiano Ronaldo made history by becoming the first player to score in six different editions of the FIFA World Cup. The 41-year-old will hope to secure Portugal a knockout stage berth, with the squad facing Colombia for their final group stage match on Saturday.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Portugal and record-breaking Ronaldo outshine Uzbekistan
items.standfirst: Record-breaker Cristiano Ronaldo guided Portugal to a 5-0 win over Uzbekistan in World Cup Group K on Tuesday. Ronaldo became ​the first player to score in six ​World Cups and Portugal’s all-time leading marksman at the global finals with two goals in Houston.
items.content: Record-breaker Cristiano Ronaldo guided Portugal to a 5-0 win over Uzbekistan in World Cup Group K on Tuesday. Ronaldo became ​the first player to score in six ​World Cups and Portugal’s all-time leading marksman at the global finals with two goals in Houston.

items.title: Portugal's Ronaldo shakes off World Cup doubters, scores 2 after sluggish start
items.standfirst: At 41, Cristiano Ronaldo is one of the oldest players in the World Cup. Against the Democratic Republic of Congo he was mostly invisible and questions mounted. Against Uzbekistan, he sparkled.
items.content: At 41, Cristiano Ronaldo is one of the oldest players in the World Cup. Against the Democratic Republic of Congo he was mostly invisible and questions mounted. Against Uzbekistan, he sparkled.(Image credit: Michael Steele)

He ‌did the same for a similar policy undertaken by the US Department of Justice’s ‌executive office for immigration review that removed limits on courthouse arrests. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Alan Wilson projected to win runoff in South Carolina GOP gubernatorial primary
clusters.neutral_headline: Alan Wilson projected to win runoff in South Carolina GOP gubernatorial primary
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Politico

items.title: Alan Wilson projected to win runoff in South Carolina GOP gubernatorial primary
items.standfirst: President Trump had endorsed both candidates in the runoff.
items.content: President Trump had endorsed both candidates in the runoff.

items.title: Alan Wilson, boosted by Trump’s late co-endorsement, wins South Carolina governor runoff
items.standfirst: The president narrowly avoided another primary endorsement blunder after he initially only backed Wilson’s opponent.
items.content: President Donald Trump just saved his nearly blemish-free endorsement record from another high-profile blunder. Attorney General Alan Wilson won the Republican nomination in the GOP runoff for South Carolina governor Tuesday, defeating Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette. Trump had initially endorsed Evette in the race but added a last-minute dual endorsement of Wilson after limited public polling showed her trailing in the race in spite of Trump’s support. Trump’s decision helped him avoid a third stinging loss after his candidates fell in Georgia’s and Iowa’s recent gubernatorial primaries. “I can’t hurt one of them by only Endorsing the other, so, therefore, I am going to Endorse, for Governor of South Carolina, both Pam Evette and Alan Wilson! It’s a Wealth of Riches – With either one you can’t go wrong,” Trump wrote on Truth Social last Friday.Wilson’s campaign built momentum quickly in the brief, two-week runoff. He touted endorsements from all but one of his four former primary challengers, including conservative firebrand Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.). The attorney general also racked up endorsements from notable out-of-state Republicans like Texas Sen. Ted Cruz and West Virginia Gov. Patrick Morrisey, who both embraced Wilson before the president did. Wilson, 52, is a longtime fixture in Columbia politics, serving as South Carolina’s top law enforcement official for the last 15 years. An Iraq war veteran, he rose to brief national prominence as he oversaw the prosecution of disgraced local attorney Alex Murdaugh, who was convicted of killing his wife and son — a case that was reopened in the final days ahead of the primary. He is also the son of Rep. Joe Wilson (R-S.C.). This year’s primary is the first time South Carolinians have cast their ballots in an open race for governor since 2010, when former Gov. Nikki Haley was elected. Current Gov. Henry McMaster — the longest-serving governor in Palmetto State history — ascended to the office when Trump appointed Haley to serve as the U.S. ambassador to the United Nations during his first term. McMaster was elected again twice but is now term-limited. Wilson will face state Rep. Jermaine Johnson, who won the Democratic primary, in November. Wilson is expected to cruise to a general election win in the deeply conservative red state: South Carolinians have not elected a Democrat for governor since 1998.

clusters.title: Some promotional Polymarket content made by online creators isn't real, WSJ reports
clusters.neutral_headline: They Looked Like They Were Getting Rich on Polymarket—but None of It Was Real
bullet_summary: The newspaper found that the prediction market paid online content creators to produce videos showing them collectively winning a total of $1
Reported by CBS News, Reuters Top News

items.title: Some promotional Polymarket content made by online creators isn't real, WSJ reports
items.standfirst: Polymarket tells CBS News it is auditing its promotional content in response to a recent investigation by the Wall Street Journal. The newspaper found that the prediction market paid online content creators to produce videos showing them collectively winning a total of $1.9 million, but the Journal says those bets weren't real. One of the article's authors, Neil Mehta, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.
items.content: Polymarket tells CBS News it is auditing its promotional content in response to a recent investigation by the Wall Street Journal. The newspaper found that the prediction market paid online content creators to produce videos showing them collectively winning a total of $1.9 million, but the Journal says those bets weren't real. One of the article's authors, Neil Mehta, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

items.title: They Looked Like They Were Getting Rich on Polymarket—but None of It Was Real
items.standfirst: A WSJ investigation says Polymarket has been promoted on social media through deceptive videos made by paid creators, creating the appearance of easy profits and hype around prediction-market betting. [wsj.com]
items.content: A WSJ investigation says Polymarket has been promoted on social media through deceptive videos made by paid creators, creating the appearance of easy profits and hype around prediction-market betting. [wsj.com]

clusters.title: House gives final approval to bipartisan housing bill aimed at lowering costs
clusters.neutral_headline: House gives final approval to bipartisan housing bill aimed at lowering costs
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, CBS News

items.title: House gives final approval to bipartisan housing bill aimed at lowering costs
items.standfirst: Donald Trump has indicated he will sign rare bipartisan initiative to tackle affordability after 358-32 vote in HouseThe House gave final approval on Tuesday to a broad bipartisan bill aimed at lowering the cost of housing, with lawmakers in both parties eager to show progress on affordability issues ahead of this year’s midterm elections.The 358-32 vote sends the bill to Donald Trump, who is expected to sign it into law on Wednesday at the Capitol. The Senate passed the legislation 85-5 on Monday. Continue reading...
items.content: Donald Trump has indicated he will sign rare bipartisan initiative to tackle affordability after 358-32 vote in HouseThe House gave final approval on Tuesday to a broad bipartisan bill aimed at lowering the cost of housing, with lawmakers in both parties eager to show progress on affordability issues ahead of this year’s midterm elections.The 358-32 vote sends the bill to Donald Trump, who is expected to sign it into law on Wednesday at the Capitol. The Senate passed the legislation 85-5 on Monday. Continue reading...

items.title: House approves major housing affordability bill, sending measure to Trump
items.standfirst: The legislation aims to increase housing supply and lower costs. It marks a rare bipartisan legislative accomplishment for lawmakers.
items.content: The legislation aims to increase housing supply and lower costs. It marks a rare bipartisan legislative accomplishment for lawmakers.

items.title: UN begins evacuation of 11,000 sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: The UN's maritime agency launched an operation to evacuate more than 11,000 sailors stranded in the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: The UN's maritime agency launched an operation to evacuate more than 11,000 sailors stranded in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: UN says it will evacuate sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz, as Rubio warns against tolls
items.standfirst: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned Iran that no country can charge fees for ships to travel through the strait.
items.content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned Iran that no country can charge fees for ships to travel through the strait.

items.title: At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion
items.standfirst: The city's main liquified natural gas (LNG) processing site suffered a "technical accident" in the Ras Laffan industrial zone.
items.content: The city's main liquified natural gas (LNG) processing site suffered a "technical accident" in the Ras Laffan industrial zone.

items.title: Qatar gas facility explosion kills 13 and leaves dozens injured
items.standfirst: At least 13 people are dead and dozens injured after an explosion at a key Qatar gas facility previously targeted by Iranian strikes.
items.content: At least 13 people are dead and dozens injured after an explosion at a key Qatar gas facility previously targeted by Iranian strikes.
